description_long: This is a robust hammer featuring a long, straight wooden handle with a smooth, natural finish and a slightly rounded end for comfortable gripping. The head is made of dark, metallic material and is shaped in a pointed, wedge-like form on one side, with a broad, flat striking face on the opposite side. The head is securely mounted to the handle, with a hexagonal cross-section at the base of the head, providing stability and strength. The pointed end suggests it is a type of masonry or stone hammer, suitable for chipping or breaking hard surfaces. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible branding, markings, or decorative elements. The hammer appears clean and well-maintained, with no signs of wear or damage.
description: A sturdy hammer with a smooth wooden handle and a dark, pointed metal head, designed for heavy-duty striking or chipping tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the pointed, wedge-shaped metal head.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the pointed tip of the head and the hexagonal shape of the hammer's striking face.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle and the sharp, elongated point of the metal head.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden handle and the broad, flat back of the hammer head.
attribute_handle_type: straight wooden handle with a smooth, natural finish and a slightly rounded end
attribute_head_type: pointed, wedge-shaped steel head with a hexagonal base and a broad, flat striking face; resembles a masonry or stone hammer
